SEARCH
Package microsim.simulation.component.device.network

Class NetworkDevice

java.lang.Object
microsim.simulation.component.SimulationComponent
microsim.simulation.component.BusComponent
microsim.simulation.component.device.IoDevice
microsim.simulation.component.device.network.NetworkDevice
public class NetworkDevice extends IoDevice
Implements a network device that offers communication, through a hub, of words (32 bit) to all other devices. Ports are same as an UART serial device (except ADDR):
  1. TX (transmit buffer)
  2. TXRDY (transmit buffer ready, that is empty)
  3. RX (receive buffer)
  4. RXRDY (receive buffer read, that is full)
  5. ADDR (returns address of this device)
All buffers are 32 bit.

    inBuf
    Buffer of words (32 bit) this device is receiving.
    private static final List<NetworkDevice>
    instances
    List of all network device instances that need to receive what this device sends.

  • Constructor Details

    • NetworkDevice

      public NetworkDevice(Bus bus, int base, Simulation simulation)
      Instantiates network device, taking a reference to the bus it's mounted on.
      Parameters:
      bus - bus the network device is mounted on
      base - base address of network device
      simulation - simulation this network device belongs to

  • Method Details

    • send

      private void send(int data)
      Sends a data word (32 bit) to all network device instances.
      Parameters:
      data - data word to send

    • getPort

      public int getPort(int index)
      Gets network ports. Ports are as follows:
      1. TX
      2. TXRDY
      3. RX
      4. RXRDY
      5. ADDR
      Specified by:
      getPort in class IoDevice
      Parameters:
      index - index of port
      Returns:
      value port should return

    • setPort

      public void setPort(int index, int data)
      Sets network ports.
      1. TX
      2. TXRDY
      3. RX
      4. RXRDY
      5. ADDR
      Specified by:
      setPort in class IoDevice
      Parameters:
      index - not significant
      data - not significant
